inputs.label: Riser Height
inputs.businessContext: Building codes (e.g., IBC, Eurocode) specify riser height between 100-220 mm for safety and comfort.

inputs.label: Tread Depth
inputs.businessContext: Building codes require tread depth typically 200-400 mm to ensure adequate foot space.

inputs.label: Number of Steps
inputs.businessContext: Total steps in the stair flight; determined by floor height and riser height.

inputs.label: Stair Width
inputs.businessContext: Minimum width per building codes (e.g., 800 mm for residential, 1000 mm for commercial).

inputs.label: Material Type
inputs.businessContext: Material affects structural design, cost, and durability. Standards: ACI 318 for concrete, AISC for steel.

inputs.label: Unit Cost of Material
inputs.businessContext: Cost per cubic meter of chosen material; varies by region and quality.

inputs.label: Labor Rate
inputs.businessContext: Hourly wage for construction labor; includes overhead.

inputs.label: Labor Hours per Step
inputs.businessContext: Estimated labor time to construct one step; based on industry benchmarks.

inputs.label: Waste Factor
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of material waste due to cutting, errors; typical 5-10%.

inputs.label: Data Confidence
inputs.businessContext: Confidence level in input data; used for risk-adjusted outputs.

thresholds.riserHeight > 190: Riser height exceeds 190 mm; consider reducing for comfort.
thresholds.treadDepth < 250: Tread depth below 250 mm; may cause safety issues.
thresholds.wasteFactor > 10: Waste factor above 10%; review material handling processes.

hiddenLossDrivers: riserHeight > 190 ? 'High riser height may reduce comfort' : ''
treadDepth < 250 ? 'Shallow tread depth may cause safety risk' : ''
suggestedActions: If riserHeight > 190, consider increasing number of steps to reduce riser height.
If treadDepth < 250, consider increasing tread depth for safety.
If wasteFactor > 10, implement better material cutting planning.
Optimize stair width to meet code minimum to reduce material cost.
